title: refine:lexical-grammar

options.label: P1
options.author: deepseek-r1:8b
options.text:
Tokens:  
- **Parentheses**: `(` and `)`  
- **Identifier**: `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*`  
- **Literal**:  
  - Number: `[0-9]+`  
  - String: `"[^"]*"` (simplified, no escapes or control characters)  
  - Boolean: `true` / `false`  
- **Comment**: `;.*(\n|$)` (precise end-of-line or file termination)  

Whitespace is ignored. Lexing order unchanged.  

**Arguments for improvement:**  
The simplified string token regex (`"[^"]*"`) removes complex escaping and control character handling, improving machine-interpretability by making string lexing more straightforward and reducing edge cases. The comment regex now explicitly handles end-of-line or end-of-file termination, ensuring better testability. These changes maintain or improve clarity while adhering to the axioms, with no degradation in functionality, thus being strictly better under machine-interpretability and testability principles.

options.label: P2
options.author: qwen3:8b
options.text: The current lexical grammar is already optimal. It balances machine-interpretable syntax with minimal primitives, avoiding unnecessary complexity. The identifier regex is sufficient for ASCII identifiers, the string literal handles escapes and line terminators, and comments are opaque metadata. No changes improve clarity, testability, or reuse without breaking existing constructs.

options.label: P3
options.author: llama3:latest
options.text:
Tokens:

- **Parentheses**: `(\(|\))`
- **Identifier**: `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_\-]`
- **Literal**:
  - Number: `\b[0-9]+\b`
  - String: `"([^"\\]|\\.|\\n\\r\\t)*"`
  - Boolean: `true` / `false`
- **Comment**: `#.*?` (non-capturing, end-of-line optional)
- **Keyword**: `if` | `lambda` | `set` | `car` | `cdr` | `cons`

Whitespace is ignored. All tokens are lexed in order, with comments treated as opaque metadata.

This revised token definition introduces a new keyword category to explicitly distinguish built-in language constructs (e.g., `if`, `lambda`, etc.) from identifiers and literals. This distinction enables more accurate parsing and improves the overall readability of code.

options.label: P4
options.author: gemma4:latest
options.text:
Tokens:
- **Parentheses**: `(` and `)`
- **Keyword**: A predefined set of reserved identifiers (e.g., `if`, `lambda`, `car`, `cdr`, `cons`, `null?`, `define`, `true`, `false`). These tokens must be recognized as keywords and cannot be parsed as generic identifiers.
- **Identifier**: `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*` (Must not match any reserved keyword).
- **Literal**:
    - Number: `[0-9]+`
    - String: `"([^"\\]|\\.|\\n\\r\\t)*"`
    - Boolean: (`true` or `false` literals, treated as Keywords).
- **Comment**: `;.*?` (non-capturing, end-of-line optional)

Whitespace is ignored. All tokens are lexed in order, with comments treated as opaque metadata.

options.label: P5
options.author: CURRENT
options.text:
Tokens:  
- **Parentheses**: `(` and `)`  
- **Identifier**: `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*`  
- **Literal**:  
  - Number: `[0-9]+`  
  - String: `"([^"\\]|\\.|\\n\\r\\t)*"`  
  - Boolean: `true` / `false`  
- **Comment**: `;.*?` (non-capturing, end-of-line optional)  

Whitespace is ignored. All tokens are lexed in order, with comments treated as opaque metadata.

options.label: P6
options.author: mistral:7b
options.text:
Tokens:
- **Parentheses**: `(` and `)`
- **Identifier**: `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*`
- **Literal**:
  - Number: `[0-9]+(\.[0-9]*)?` or `(\.[0-9]+)?` (for floating point numbers)
  - String: `"([^"\r\n\\]|\\[bfrnrt\\/]*|\\u[0-9a-fA-F]{4})*"`
  - Boolean: `true` / `false`
- **Comment**: `; (.*|(\r?\n))*` (non-capturing, end-of-line required)
- **Keyword**: reserved words like `set`, `if`, `lambda`, `car`, `cdr`, ... are added to the lexer.

The proposed lexer now explicitly accounts for identifiers as keywords and improves the number literal pattern to cover floating point numbers more accurately, without adding unnecessary complexity or ambiguity. The comment pattern also requires an end-of-line character, making comments more predictable and easily skippable during parsing.
